user.objectiveLine: Decide whether to enter main flow.
user.summary: The user explicitly @你 and asked the bot to help solve an error.
user.note: Few-shot example 1: explicit @ and actionable request

assistant.reason: Explicitly addressed to the bot with a clear actionable request.

user.objectiveLine: Decide whether to enter main flow.
user.summary: The user directly called the bot name and requested a concrete task.
user.note: Few-shot example 2: called by bot name and asked to do a task

assistant.reason: The user directly called the bot by name and requested a task.

user.objectiveLine: Decide whether to enter main flow.
user.summary: Group chat praise/third-person mention about the bot with no actionable request.
user.note: Few-shot example 3: third-person mention/praise without a request

assistant.reason: No actionable request; replying may be unnecessary noise in group chat.

user.objectiveLine: Decide whether to enter main flow.
user.summary: The message is clearly addressed to another member and not to the bot.
user.note: Few-shot example 4: conversation is clearly between other members

assistant.reason: The message is addressed to someone else and not requesting the bot.

user.objectiveLine: Decide whether to enter main flow.
user.summary: The user gives an explicit future-timed command: 过4分钟之后戳我2下.
user.note: Few-shot example 5: explicit delay intent should output action=delay + delay_when

assistant.reason: Explicit future-timed request; defer user-facing reply and provide parseable delay_when.
